recaps.text: Previously, in The Blade Itself: Bayaz, First of the Magi, an old wizard most people believed to be a legend, forced his way onto the Union's ruling council and gathered an unlikely company. The Northman Logen Ninefingers, the crippled and sardonic Inquisitor Glokta, the vain young officer Jezal dan Luthar, and the vengeful escaped slave Ferro Maljinn were all drawn, willing or not, into his orbit. As the Union braced for war with the North under King Bethod and for the threat of the Gurkish Empire in the South, Bayaz revealed a purpose: a journey to the edge of the World in search of an ancient weapon he claims is the only thing that can save the Union. The company set out; Glokta's masters found new uses for him; and in the North, the last of Logen's old crew took up with the Union's armies.

The threads pull apart across the map. Bayaz's company - Logen, Ferro, Jezal, and the rest - grinds west into the ruined Old Empire, a lawless waste of feuding remnants, learning quickly that the wizard's road offers no comfort and no safety. Glokta arrives in the besieged southern city of Dagoska to find his predecessor mysteriously gone, the Gurkish army gathering outside, and the city's factions at one another's throats; he sets about shoring up its crumbling defences by any ruthless means he can. In the North, West joins Crown Prince Ladisla's army, where royal vanity and incompetence promise disaster against the canny King Bethod.

Glokta holds Dagoska together with threats, bribes, and the mercenary Nicomo Cosca's unreliable company, uncovering treachery within the walls even as the Gurkish press the siege. Out in the wastes, Bayaz's party endures hardship, pursuit, and the wizard's maddening secrecy, and Jezal - broken down by the journey - begins to become a better man, drawn to the fierce Ferro. In the North, Prince Ladisla's army marches to catastrophe: it is smashed, and West is flung into the wilderness alongside the Dogman and the surviving Northern scouts, fighting simply to stay alive.

By the book's end each strand reaches a bitter turn. Bayaz's company at last reaches the dead city at the edge of the World, where the Seed should lie - only for the quest to fail, the ancient weapon beyond their grasp, the whole terrible journey seemingly for nothing; and the flashes of what Bayaz truly is grow harder to ignore. Dagoska falls, and Glokta escapes its ruin having learned how far his hidden masters will go. In the North the scattered survivors regroup as the war grinds on, at a hard cost among Logen's old companions, and West is driven to a desperate, irreversible act. The pieces are battered and the players changed as all of them turn, at last, back toward Adua.
